subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Rate & Accumulation (Riemann Sums)
stimulus:
People enter a line for an escalator at a rate modeled by the function r given by

where r ( t ) is measured in people per second and t is measured in seconds. As people get on the escalator,
they exit the line at a constant rate of 0.7 person per second. There are 20 people in line at time t = 0.

parts.prompt: How many people enter the line for the escalator during the time interval 0 £ t £ 30 0 ?

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (a), point 1
See College Board scoring guidelines for part (a), point 2

parts.prompt:
During the time interval 0 £ t £ 30 0, there are always people in line for the escalator. How many people
are in line at time t = 300 ?

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (b), point 1
See College Board scoring guidelines for part (b), point 2

parts.prompt: For t > 300, what is the first time t that there are no people in line for the escalator?

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (c), point 1

parts.prompt:
For 0 £ t £ 30 0, at what time t is the number of people in line a minimum? To the nearest whole
number, find the number of people in line at this time. Justify your answer.
